title: Rattle Source Mapping
description: Output of the rattle-ingest workflow — the first link in the chain rattle-ingest → rattle-pricelist-analysis → rattle-suggest-config → rattle-apply-config. A REVIEWABLE map from an arbitrary customer source file (Excel / CSV / ERP export / PDF pricelist) onto Rattle entities. It carries NO configuration decisions: only the detected sheet shape, one mapping per source column, every column that could not be mapped, and the blockers a human must answer before rattle-suggest-config may run. Never fabricate a standard option, a price, or a part number — emit a placeholder plus a blocker instead.

tenant.description: Tenant slug (matches the RATTLE_API_KEY_<TENANT> convention). Optional — ingestion is a local, read-only step.

columns.description: One entry per source column that received a role. Every column of the source file MUST appear either here or in `unmapped_columns` — never silently drop a column.

unmapped_columns.description: Columns deliberately left unmapped. Presence here is the audit trail proving the column was seen and considered.

blockers.description: Hard stops. While this array is non-empty, rattle-suggest-config MUST NOT be run: the source does not contain enough information to build a correct BOM-aware configuration. Each blocker states the placeholder that was emitted in place of a fabricated value and the question a human must answer.

warnings.description: Soft findings — low-confidence mappings, coerced values, assumed separators. Non-blocking, but every one of them is a review item.

normalized_rows_path.description: Path to the emitted normalized-rows.json (a JSON array of flat objects). That array is the input to skills/rattle-pricelist-analysis/scripts/detect_anti_patterns.py and to rattle-suggest-config.

notes.description: Free-form remarks for the human reviewer (assumptions, provenance, follow-ups).

decimal_string.description: Backend serialises prices as decimal strings (Numeric(10,2) → string). Accept either numeric or canonical 2-decimal string. Mirrors recommendation.schema.json#/$defs/decimal_string.

confidence.description: 0.00–1.00. < 0.60 MUST also raise a `low-confidence-mapping` warning and set `review_required: true`.

source.description: Provenance of the ingested file. Reading is delegated to rattle_api.source.read_source (Excel/PDF/Word) or the stdlib csv/json readers — rattle-ingest does not reimplement file parsing.

sheet_name.description: Worksheet actually profiled. Null for non-sheet formats.

sheet_names.description: All worksheets present. More than one raises a `multiple-sheets-present` warning — ingest one sheet per mapping.

header_row.description: 0-based index of the row treated as the header. Anything other than 0 raises an `assumed-header-row` warning.

checksum_sha256.description: Hex SHA-256 of the source bytes. Lets a later re-ingest prove the source did not change.

sheet_shape.description: The detected shape of the sheet. Each id has exactly one documented normalization strategy — see skills/rattle-ingest/references/sheet-shapes.md.

evidence.description: Deterministic signals that drove the guess (cardinality, dtype, header shape) — never the header alone.

candidates.description: Ranked runners-up. Two candidates within 0.15 of each other raise a `sheet-shape-ambiguous` warning.

normalization.description: One-line restatement of the normalization strategy applied (e.g. 'pivot variant columns into (group, option) pairs; one normalized row per cell').

column_role.description: The 24 column roles. Full detection heuristics: skills/rattle-ingest/references/column-roles.md.

target_entity.description: Rattle entity the column feeds. `none` = the column informs parsing or review but is never written (e.g. `currency`, which ProductCreateRequest accepts but IGNORES — currency is derived from the company base price list).

transform.description: Deterministic cell transform applied on normalization. `decimal_de` parses '1.234,56 €' → '1234.56'; `decimal_en` parses '1,234.56' → '1234.56'; `boolean_marker` maps x / ✓ / • / ja / yes → true; `standard_marker` maps Serie / serienmäßig / Standard / S → recommended=true; `header_to_option_name` and `cell_to_option_price` are the wide-variant-matrix pivot pair.

index.description: 0-based column index in the source sheet.

header.description: Verbatim header cell. May be empty — an empty header is not a reason to drop the column.

target_field.description: Field on the target entity. MUST be a field that exists on the backend create/update schema (e.g. option.price, part.part_number, option.number_unit). Rattle has NO Product.sku field — carry a customer article number in product.integration_metadata.<key>.

transform_arg.description: Argument for the transform (e.g. the constant value, the list separator, the integration_metadata key).

derived.description: Values the mapping ATTACHES to every cell of this column rather than reading from it. Required for wide-variant-matrix pivots, where the header is the option name and the group must be supplied by the reviewer.

dtype.description: Deterministic dtype guess from the VALUES, not the header.

samples.description: Up to 5 verbatim value samples — the evidence a reviewer needs to confirm or reject the role.

candidates.description: Ranked alternative roles the profiler considered.

review_required.description: True whenever confidence < 0.60, or the mapping invents structure the source does not state (a pivot's group_name, for example).

reason.description: Why no role applied. 'Unknown' is an acceptable reason; silence is not.

suggestion.description: What a human could do with it (e.g. 'internal cost column — confirm it is not customer-facing before discarding').

blocker.description: A hard stop. Ingestion NEVER invents the missing value: it emits a placeholder and records the question. `pattern_id` is present whenever the blocker is an instance of a catalogued anti-pattern (skills/rattle-configurator/references/anti-patterns.md).

pattern_id.description: Anti-pattern id from skills/rattle-configurator/references/anti-patterns.md (mirrored in rattle_api.knowledge.ANTI_PATTERNS and skills/rattle-pricelist-analysis/scripts/detect_anti_patterns.py). Set it whenever the blocker is an instance of a catalogued anti-pattern — the downstream rattle-pricelist-analysis report indexes on it, so the two findings merge cleanly. Keep this enum in sync with the catalogue.

location.description: Pointer into this document or the source (e.g. 'columns[3]', "Preisliste!D1", 'rows[7].Aufpreis').

evidence.description: Verbatim cell / header text that triggered the blocker.

placeholder.description: The placeholder emitted INSTEAD of a fabricated value, e.g. 'PLACEHOLDER: 17 Zoll (standard variant — unconfirmed)'. Never a guessed price, name, or part number.

question.description: The single question a human must answer to clear the blocker, e.g. 'Which wheel size ships as standard, and at what BOM cost?'
